questions.stem: Statements: All squares are rectangles. All rectangles are parallelograms. No parallelogram is a triangle. Conclusions: I. No square is a triangle. II. Some rectangles are not triangles.
questions.solution: Both follow

questions.solution: 14th

questions.stem: Synonym of HARMONY:

questions.stem: The new method is more ____ than the old one.
questions.solution: efficient

questions.stem: The accident ____ a review of safety protocols.

questions.stem: The government ____ the emergency protocol yesterday.

questions.stem: Synonym of RIGOROUS:

questions.stem: The government is ____ to attract investment.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Meaning of 'To be on cloud nine':
questions.solution: To be extremely happy

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Correct: He is suffering from fever since last week.
options: He suffers from fever since last week.
He has been suffering from fever since last week.
He was suffering from fever since last week.
He is suffering from fever since last week.
questions.solution: He has been suffering from fever since last week.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Antonym of INNOCUOUS:
questions.solution: Harmful

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Correct: Neither the manager nor his assistants was present.
options: Neither the manager nor his assistants are being present.
Neither the manager nor his assistants has been present.
Neither the manager nor his assistants were present.
Neither the manager nor his assistants is present.
questions.solution: Neither the manager nor his assistants were present.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Meaning of 'Bite the bullet':
questions.solution: To endure a painful situation

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Correct: He asked that whether I could help him.
options: He asked that whether I could help him.
He asked if whether I could help him.
He asked that I could help him.
He asked whether I could help him.
questions.solution: He asked whether I could help him.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Meaning of 'To cost an arm and a leg':
questions.solution: To be very expensive

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Synonym of OSTENTATIOUS:
questions.solution: Showy

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A PERT activity has to = 2, tm = 6, tp = 10 days. Its expected time is:

questions.subject: Industrial Engineering
questions.stem: Karl Newbauer's model relates:
questions.solution: Newbauer: learning curve. Y = aX^b. Time decreases by fixed % each doubling of cumulative output. Power law.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: ARIMA (Autoregressive Integrated Moving Average):
options: AR(p): y_t = c + φ₁y_{t-1}+...+ε_t. MA(q): y_t = ε_t + θ₁ε_{t-1}+... I(d): differencing. Box-Jenkins methodology
questions.solution: ARIMA(p,d,q): identification (ACF/PACF) → estimation → diagnostic check. ARIMA(1,1,1) = differenced ARMA.

questions.subject: Industrial Engineering
questions.stem: Total Productive Maintenance (TPM) 8 pillars include:
options: Autonomous maintenance, planned maintenance, quality maintenance
questions.solution: TPM pillars: autonomous, planned, quality, focused improvement, early mgmt, training, safety, office TPM.

questions.subject: Industrial Engineering
questions.stem: Pugh concept selection matrix:
options: Evaluates concepts against criteria and baseline
questions.solution: Pugh: +, -, S (same) vs baseline. Sum +'s and -'s. Iterate to improve concepts. Simple but effective.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: World-class OEE benchmarks:
options: Availability > 90%, Performance > 95%, Quality > 99.9%. OEE > 85% = world class
questions.solution: OEE < 65%: low. 65-75%: typical. 75-85%: good. > 85%: excellent. Big 6 losses.

questions.subject: Industrial Engineering
questions.stem: MRP nervousness refers to:
options: Sensitivity of MRP to small changes in input
questions.solution: Nervousness: small demand changes cause large schedule changes. Caused by lot-sizing, lead time, BOM changes. Dampening methods help.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Production Order Quantity (POQ):
questions.solution: POQ: for manufacturing. p = production rate, d = demand rate. Q_p > EOQ.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Two components each with reliability 0.9 in parallel give a system reliability of:

questions.subject: Industrial Engineering
questions.stem: OSHA standards:
options: Set permissible exposure limits for workplace hazards
questions.solution: OSHA PELs: legal limits for worker exposure.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: On the break-even chart shown (fixed cost ₹30000, price ₹100, variable cost ₹40/unit), the marked point where revenue meets total cost occurs at:
questions.solution: BEP = FC/(p − v) = 30000/(100 − 40) = 500 units, where total revenue = total cost.

figure.caption: Break-even chart

questions.subject: Industrial Engineering
questions.stem: Economic Value Added (EVA):
options: Net operating profit after tax minus capital charge
questions.solution: EVA = NOPAT - (WACC × Invested Capital). Positive EVA = value creation. Measures true economic profit.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Mean Absolute Scaled Error (MASE):
options: Scaled by in-sample MAE of naive forecast. <1 = better than naive. For comparing across series
questions.solution: MASE: MAE / MAE_naive (in-sample). Not sensitive to scale. Can compare between series.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Facility layout — QAP formulation:
options: Min Σ Σ Σ Σ f_ik · d_jl · x_ij · x_kl. Quadratic assignment. NP-hard. Heuristics solve moderate
questions.solution: QAP: assign facilities to locations. f = flow, d = distance. Solved by branch-and-bound for n < 30.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Cognitive ergonomics: human error types:
options: Slip (wrong action), lapse (memory failure), mistake (wrong plan), violation (intentional)
questions.solution: Slip: correct plan, wrong execution. Mistake: wrong plan. Violation: intentional deviation.

questions.subject: Industrial Engineering
questions.stem: Bottleneck shift after exploiting constraint:
questions.solution: After elevating one constraint, another may become bottleneck. Repeat entire cycle. Continuous improvement.

questions.subject: Industrial Engineering
questions.stem: FMEA in design phase identifies:
options: Potential failure modes and their effects
questions.solution: Design FMEA: proactive. Identify what can go wrong, effects, causes. RPN prioritizes design changes.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Theory of Constraints — throughput metrics:
options: Throughput (T) = sales - raw materials. Inventory (I) = total invested. Operating expense (OE) = all costs to convert I to T
questions.solution: Goldratt: goal = increase T - OE. T/I = productivity. $days = I × days in system.

questions.subject: Industrial Engineering
questions.stem: Total Productive Maintenance (TPM) availability:
questions.solution: TPM availability: A = (run time - downtime) / planned production time. Component of OEE (Overall Equipment Effectiveness).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Work sampling: proportion of idle time p=0.3, e=0.05, 95% confidence:

questions.subject: Industrial Engineering
questions.stem: Constraint propagation in CSP: reduces variable domains by:
questions.solution: Arc consistency: for each value, ensure supporting value exists in other variable. Reduces search space.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Acceptance sampling: AQL (Acceptable Quality Level):
options: Maximum percent defective that is considered acceptable as a process average
questions.solution: AQL: quality level consumer considers acceptable. Producer's risk (alpha) at AQL.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: VE evaluation phase — weighted matrix:
options: Weighted criteria × score = total. Compare alternatives against functions. Matrix shows best value option
questions.solution: Evaluation: criteria weighted (1-10). Score: 1-10 per alternative. Total = Σ(w_i × s_i).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Queuing — M/D/1:
options: Constant service time. L_q = ρ²/(2(1-ρ)). Half of M/M/1, lower than M/G/1
questions.solution: M/D/1: deterministic service. P-K formula. L = λ·E[S] + λ²E[S²]/(2(1-ρ)).

questions.subject: Industrial Engineering
questions.stem: ABC inventory analysis classifies items by:
questions.solution: ABC: A=high value (80/20), B=medium, C=low value.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: An assembly line has total task time 54 min, 6 stations and a cycle time of 12 min. Its balance efficiency is:
questions.solution: Efficiency = Σt/(N·C) = 54/(6×12) = 75%.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Process layout:
options: Grouped by function (all lathes together, all mills together). Flexible route
questions.solution: Process: job shop scheduling. Material handling cost high. Complex routing.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Theory of Constraints: five focusing steps:
questions.solution: TOC: focus on bottleneck. Don't optimize non-bottleneck. Goldratt: Goal, Constraints, Throughput Accounting.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Depreciation methods:
options: Straight line (SL), declining balance (DB), sum-of-years-digits (SYD), MACRS
questions.solution: SL: equal annual. DB: accelerated. MACRS: IRS guidelines for tax. Salvage value.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Critical Path Method: total float = LS - ES = LF - EF. Activities on critical path have:
questions.solution: Critical path: longest path. Float = 0. Any delay delays project.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Quality function deployment (QFD):
options: House of Quality. Translate customer voice into design requirements
questions.solution: QFD: customer requirements (Whats) vs technical characteristics (Hows). Prioritization.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: With demand 2000/yr, order cost ₹50 and order quantity 400, the annual ordering cost is:
questions.solution: Annual ordering cost = (D/Q)×S = (2000/400)×50 = ₹250.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Adding components in parallel (redundancy) generally:
questions.solution: Parallel redundancy means the system works if any one path works, raising reliability.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: VE — cost model:
options: Pareto on costs (80/20). Target costs for each function. Function-cost matrix
questions.solution: Cost model: identify high-cost functions. Focus VE on 20% of parts that represent 80% of cost.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: An assembly line has a cycle time of 2 min and runs for 400 min/shift. The shift output is:
questions.solution: Output = available time / cycle time = 400/2 = 200 units.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: An assembly line has total task time 54 min, 5 stations and a cycle time of 12 min. Its balance efficiency is:
questions.solution: Efficiency = Σt/(N·C) = 54/(5×12) = 90%.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: An assembly line has a cycle time of 3 min and runs for 420 min/shift. The shift output is:
questions.solution: Output = available time / cycle time = 420/3 = 140 units.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: An assembly line has a cycle time of 5 min and runs for 480 min/shift. The shift output is:
questions.solution: Output = available time / cycle time = 480/5 = 96 units.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Johnson's rule is used to sequence n jobs through:
options: Any number of machines for profit
questions.solution: Johnson's rule gives the optimal job sequence on two machines minimizing total completion time.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: MODI method: u_i + v_j = c_ij for basic cells. For non-basic cell (i,j), optimality requires:
questions.solution: Optimality: all reduced costs non-negative (min). If any Δ<0, not optimal. Enter that variable.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Earned Value: CPI = EV/AC < 1 means:
questions.solution: CPI < 1: cost overrun. CPI > 1: cost underrun. SPI < 1: behind schedule.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Aggregate planning:
options: Medium-term (3-18 months). Demand/supply matching. Level, chase, mixed strategies
questions.solution: Level: constant production, inventory buffer. Chase: vary workforce. Mixed: combination.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Which statement about an LP feasible region is correct?
options: The optimum lies at the centre
It is convex, and the optimum lies at a vertex
questions.solution: LP feasible regions are convex polytopes; an optimum (if it exists) occurs at a corner point.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A worker's observed time is 4 min at a performance rating of 80%. The normal time is:
questions.solution: Normal time = observed × rating = 4×80% = 3.2 min.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Fixed cost ₹20000, price ₹100, variable cost ₹60/unit. The output needed for a target profit of ₹20000 is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Average Run Length (ARL):
options: Expected number of points before out-of-control signal. ARL₀ (in-control) = 1/α
questions.solution: ARL₀ = 370 for 3σ limits. ARL₁ depends on shift size. Lower ARL₁ = faster detection.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Condition-based maintenance: oil analysis detects:
options: Wear metal particles, contamination, degradation of oil properties
questions.solution: Oil analysis: spectrometric (Fe, Cu, Pb), particle count, viscosity, TBN. Wear debris analysis.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Closeness rating in SLP: X (undesirable) means:
options: Departments should NOT be placed near each other (e.g., noisy next to quiet)
questions.solution: X: avoid placing together. E.g., paint shop near assembly (fumes), warehouse near office (noise).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Seasonal indices — ratio-to-moving-average:
options: Divide by centered MA. Average seasonal ratios. Adjust to sum to 4 (quarterly)
questions.solution: Seasonal: 12 months or 4 quarters. Centered MA of order 12 or 4. Normalize indices.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Supply chain network design: number and location of facilities:
options: Trade-off between transportation cost, inventory cost, and service level
questions.solution: More facilities: higher inventory, lower transport, better service. Fewer: opposite.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Annual demand = 5000 units, ordering cost = ₹50/order, holding cost = ₹5/unit/yr. The EOQ is about:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: For the CPM network shown (path 1-2-4 = A+B = 4+6, path 1-3-4 = C+D = 2+10), the project duration (critical path) is:
questions.solution: Path durations: 1-2-4 = 10, 1-3-4 = 12. The critical path is the longest = 12 days.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: PPC functions:
questions.solution: PPC: planning (routing, scheduling, loading) + control (dispatching, follow-up).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The process capability index Cpk, unlike Cp, accounts for:
options: Process centring relative to the specification limits
questions.solution: Cpk penalizes off-centre processes; Cp assumes perfect centring and uses only the spread.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Service level (cycle):
options: Probability of not stocking out during lead time
questions.solution: CSL = P(demand during LT ≤ ROP). 95% CSL: 5% chance of stockout per cycle.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Process capability: Cp = (USL-LSL)/(6σ). For USL-LSL=4, σ=0.5:
questions.solution: Cp: potential capability. Cp = 1.33: process fits spec. Cp > 1.67: excellent. Cp < 1: incapable.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Reverse logistics:
options: Returns, recycling, remanufacturing, disposal. Closed-loop supply chain
questions.solution: Reverse: product take-back. Value recovery. Remanufacturing: like-new condition (automotive parts).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Control chart — CUSUM:
options: Cumulative sum of deviations from target. V-mask. Detects small shifts
questions.solution: CUSUM: S_i = max(0, S_{i-1} + x_i - μ_0 - k). Decision interval h. Tabular form.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Safety stock: z=2.33, σ_L=15. SS=

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Quality circles:
options: Small group of workers solving work-related problems. Voluntarily, regularly
questions.solution: Quality circle: 5-10 members. Identify, analyze, solve problems. Empower employees.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Configure-to-order (CTO):
options: Standard modules configured to customer. Dell, car configurators. Postponement
questions.solution: CTO: mass customization. Modular design. Configurator rules. Lead time reduction.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: SPC: control chart signals out of control when:
options: Points outside control limits or non-random patterns (trends, cycles, shifts)
questions.solution: Western Electric rules: 1 point >3σ, 2/3 >2σ same side, 4/5 >1σ same side, 8 consecutive same side.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Supplier relationship management: dual sourcing:
options: Two suppliers per component (reduces risk vs single, more manageable than multi)
questions.solution: Dual: 70/30 or 80/20 split. Competition + security. Single: higher risk, lower cost.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: With α = 0.3, last actual = 250 and last forecast = 280, the next exponential-smoothing forecast is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Daily demand = 80, lead time = 6 days, safety stock = 50 units. The reorder point is:
questions.solution: ROP = (demand × lead time) + safety stock = 80×6 + 50 = 530 units.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Reliability — component vs system:
options: Series: R_s = ΠR_i. Parallel: R_p = 1 - Π(1-R_i). k-out-of-n: binomial
questions.solution: k-out-of-n: at least k of n working. Binary reliability. RBD (reliability block diagram).

questions.subject: Industrial Engineering
questions.stem: Seasonal index of 1.25 means:
options: That month typically 25 percent above average
questions.solution: Seasonal index: 1.0 = average. >1 above average.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Acceptance sampling: consumer's risk (β) is:
options: Probability of accepting a bad lot (Type II error)
questions.solution: β: P(accept | lot is bad). AQL: acceptable quality level (α risk). LTPD: lot tolerance percent defective (β risk).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The last three demands were 100, 150 and 120 units. The 3-period moving-average forecast is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: PERT: expected time t_e = (a+4m+b)/6. Variance = ((b-a)/6)^2. For a=5, m=8, b=17:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A line makes 400 units using 40 labour-hours. The labour productivity is:
questions.solution: Productivity = output/input = 400/40 = 10 units/hour.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Reliability bathtub curve — infant mortality:
options: High initial failures. Causes: defects, misassembly. Burn-in test eliminates
questions.solution: Infant mortality: decreasing failure rate. Burn-in: 24-168 hours. Environmental stress screening (ESS).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: On the inventory cost curve shown (D = 2000, S = ₹50, H = ₹4), the order size Q* that minimizes total cost (where ordering and holding costs are equal) is:
questions.solution: Total cost is minimum at the EOQ Q* = √(2DS/H) = √(2×2000×50/4) ≈ 223.6 units, where the two cost curves cross.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Mean Squared Error (MSE):
options: Average of (actual - forecast)² over n periods
questions.solution: MSE: Σe_t²/n. Penalizes large errors more than MAD.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: On the inventory cost curve shown (D = 4000, S = ₹50, H = ₹5), the order size Q* that minimizes total cost (where ordering and holding costs are equal) is:
questions.solution: Total cost is minimum at the EOQ Q* = √(2DS/H) = √(2×4000×50/5) ≈ 282.8 units, where the two cost curves cross.

figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 260 140" width="260" height="140"><line x1="30" y1="120" x2="240" y2="120" stroke="#94a3b8" stroke-width="1"/><line x1="30" y1="15" x2="30" y2="120" stroke="#94a3b8" stroke-width="1"/><path d="M40 25 C 90 90 110 100 240 112" fill="none" stroke="#16a34a" stroke-width="1.5"/><text x="60" y="40" font-size="8" fill="#16a34a">ordering</text><line x1="35" y1="118" x2="235" y2="40" stroke="#dc2626" stroke-width="1.5"/><text x="190" y="55" font-size="8" fill="#dc2626">holding</text><path d="M40 60 C 110 30 120 30 235 95" fill="none" stroke="#2563eb" stroke-width="2"/><text x="120" y="28" font-size="8" fill="#2563eb">total cost</text><line x1="120" y1="48" x2="120" y2="120" stroke="#475569" stroke-width="1" stroke-dasharray="3 2"/><text x="110" y="134" font-size="8" fill="#475569">Q*</text></svg>

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A PERT activity has to = 4 and tp = 14 days. Its standard deviation is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A PERT activity has to = 4, tm = 8, tp = 10 days. Its expected time is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: An activity's normal time/cost are 10 days/₹3000 and crash time/cost are 5 days/₹5000. Its crash cost slope is:
questions.solution: Cost slope = (crash − normal cost)/(normal − crash time) = (5000−3000)/(10−5) = ₹400/day.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Monte Carlo simulation in project scheduling:
options: Triangular/PERT distribution for activity durations. Run 1000+ iterations. P50, P80, P90 project duration
questions.solution: Monte Carlo: @Risk, Crystal Ball. P80 = 80% confidence to complete. Schedule reserve.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Sensitivity analysis — shadow price:
options: Change in objective per unit change in RHS (within allowable range). Dual variable
questions.solution: Shadow price: useful for resource valuation. If shadow price > market price → buy more.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Network flow: max-flow min-cut theorem:
options: Maximum flow from source to sink equals minimum capacity of cut separating source from sink
questions.solution: Min-cut: set of edges whose removal disconnects source from sink. Max flow = min cut capacity.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: CPM — free float:
options: FF = ES_next - EF_current. Time an activity can delay without affecting next activity
questions.solution: FF: = min(ES of successors) - EF. FF ≤ TF. Zero on critical path.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Queuing system components:
options: Arrival process, queue discipline (FIFO, priority), service process, servers
questions.solution: Kendall notation: A/B/s/K/N/D. M/M/1: Markovian arrival/service, 1 server, infinite queue.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Ishikawa (fishbone) diagram categories (6M):
options: Man, Machine, Material, Method, Measurement, Mother Nature (Environment)
questions.solution: Fishbone: 6M categories. Brainstorming tool. Systematic cause identification.

questions.subject: Industrial Engineering
questions.stem: Capital recovery factor converts:
options: Present value to equivalent annual cost
questions.solution: CRF = i(1+i)^n / ((1+i)^n - 1). Used for annualized cost of capital investments. Standard engineering economy tool.

questions.subject: Industrial Engineering
questions.stem: Type I error in SPC:
options: False alarm when process in control
questions.solution: Type I: false alarm. Type II: missed signal.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Condition monitoring: vibration analysis detects:
options: Imbalance, misalignment, bearing defects, gear faults
questions.solution: Vibration: FFT spectrum. 1×=imbalance, 2×=misalignment, bearing frequencies (BPFO, BPFI).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Traveling salesman problem (TSP):
options: Find minimum tour visiting n cities once. Held-Karp DP O(n²·2ⁿ). LKH (Lin-Kernighan) heuristic for large n
questions.solution: TSP: Euclidean, metric, asymmetric. 2-opt, 3-opt local search. Concorde (TSP solver) uses B&C.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: For D = 3600/yr, S = ₹80, H = ₹2/unit/yr, the minimum total ordering-plus-holding cost (at EOQ ≈ 536.7) is about:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Transportation problem: a basic feasible solution for m sources × n destinations has at most:
questions.solution: Transportation: m+n-1 basic cells. Non-basic = m×n-(m+n-1) cells at zero.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: 5S: Sort, Set in order, Shine, Standardize, Sustain:
questions.solution: 5S: Japanese workplace organization. Reduces waste, improves safety, increases efficiency.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Multivariate SPC — Hotelling T²:
options: Monitor multiple correlated variables simultaneously. χ² / F distribution for UCL. Phase I vs Phase II
questions.solution: Hotelling T²: T² = n(x̄-μ₀)'Σ⁻¹(x̄-μ₀). Special cause decomposition. MEWMA, MCUSUM extensions.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: With α = 0.3, last actual = 300 and last forecast = 180, the next exponential-smoothing forecast is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Sales and Operations Planning (S&OP):
options: Monthly review. Demand plan, supply plan, financials. Executive meeting
questions.solution: S&OP: cross-functional. Four phases: data, demand, supply, pre-meeting, executive S&OP.

questions.subject: Industrial Engineering
questions.stem: Economic Order Quantity with quantity discount:
options: Compare total cost at each price break
questions.solution: Quantity discount: total cost curve shifts at each break. Compare TC at EOQ and each break point. Choose minimum.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: In acceptance sampling, a Type I error (producer's risk) means:
questions.solution: Type I (α): a genuinely acceptable lot is rejected; Type II (β): a bad lot is accepted.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: On the Pareto chart of defect causes shown (tallest bar = cause B), the principle being applied says you should first address:
options: Cause B (the tallest bar — the vital few)
questions.solution: The Pareto (80/20) principle prioritizes the 'vital few' tallest bars; cause B contributes the most defects and is tackled first.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Warehouse slotting optimization:
options: Class-based turnover: fast movers (golden zone, waist level). Pareto: 20% SKU = 80% orders
questions.solution: Slotting: cube movement (pick frequency × cube). Forward pick area replenishment. Batch size.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: FMEA — RPN interpretation:
options: S (severity 1-10), O (occurrence 1-10), D (detection 1-10). RPN = S×O×D. RPN > 100 = action required
questions.solution: FMEA: risk priority number. Severity: 9-10 (safety). Occurrence: 10 (very high). Detection: 10 (undetectable).

questions.subject: Industrial Engineering
questions.stem: Poka-Yoke (mistake-proofing) examples include:
options: Fixtures, guides, sensors that prevent errors
questions.solution: Poka-yoke: physical device prevents defects. Examples: asymmetric pins, guide rails, sensors. Eliminates human error.
